courses.title: Applied Psychology
outcomes: Explain core psychological concepts and their relevance to nursing practice.
Relate human development, cognition, emotion and behaviour to health and illness.
Apply psychological principles when communicating with patients, families and healthcare teams.
Recognize common responses to stress, loss, hospitalization and chronic illness.
Use culturally sensitive approaches that support coping, resilience and therapeutic relationships.

courses.title: Medical Surgical Nursing II
outcomes: Apply integrated nursing and clinical sciences when caring for adults with complex medical-surgical conditions.
Use clinical reasoning and the nursing process to plan safe, holistic and evidence-informed care.
Recognize deterioration, prioritize nursing actions and communicate concerns using structured tools.
Plan care for patients with cardiovascular, respiratory, neurological, endocrine and musculoskeletal disorders.
Provide safe perioperative nursing care and patient education across the continuum of care.
Demonstrate ethical, culturally sensitive and collaborative practice in clinical settings.

courses.title: Health Assessment II
outcomes: Perform focused and comprehensive assessment of major body systems.
Differentiate expected, abnormal and urgent assessment findings.
Integrate history, physical examination and available clinical data.
Document and communicate assessment findings accurately and professionally.
Use assessment findings to support nursing priorities and timely escalation.

courses.title: Pathophysiology II
outcomes: Explain altered physiological mechanisms affecting major body systems.
Relate cellular and systemic dysfunction to clinical manifestations and complications.
Connect pathophysiological processes with assessment findings and nursing priorities.
Compare acute and chronic patterns of disease progression.
Apply pathophysiology principles to safe patient education and clinical reasoning.

courses.title: Clinical Pharmacology and Drug Administration II
outcomes: Explain the actions, indications, contraindications and major adverse effects of commonly used drug classes.
Apply pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic principles to safe medication administration.
Recognize clinically significant interactions, high-alert risks and adverse drug reactions.
Use assessment and monitoring data to evaluate therapeutic and harmful medication effects.
Provide accurate patient education while avoiding unsupported dosing or prescribing advice.

courses.title: Professional Ethics for Nurses
outcomes: Explain ethical principles, professional values and codes relevant to nursing.
Apply structured ethical reasoning to common nursing dilemmas.
Protect dignity, privacy, confidentiality, autonomy and informed decision-making.
Recognize professional boundaries, accountability and responsibilities in digital and clinical practice.
Communicate and escalate ethical concerns through appropriate professional channels.
